title_en: Women’s progress at work is stalling
title_zh: 女性在职场的进展正在停滞
rubric_en: Is the “Lean In” generation leaning out?
rubric_zh: “Lean In（挺身而出]”一代正在“lean out”吗？

paragraphs.en: WOMEN HAVE made extraordinary gains in the past few decades across the rich world. They now outnumber men on university campuses in virtually every wealthy country. At work, many have been enthusiastically “leaning in”, as Sheryl Sandberg, then Facebook’s second-in-command, urged them to do in a book in 2013. Their representation among the highest-paying professions, including medicine and the law, has nearly tripled in America since 1980. At the turn of the century a British patient was twice as likely to see a man. Last year the number of female physicians exceeded those of men for the first time, according to Britain’s General Medical Council, a public body.
paragraphs.zh: 过去几十年里，女性在富裕国家取得了非凡进步。几乎在每个富裕国家，大学校园里女性人数都已超过男性。在职场中，许多人热情地“lean in”（挺身而入），正如2013年时Facebook副总裁之一谢丽尔·桑德伯格在其著作中所倡导的。自1980年以来，女性在高薪职业中的比例——包括医学和法律——在美国几乎增长了三倍。世纪之交时，一名英国患者看到男性医生的概率是看到女性医生的两倍。根据英国医学总委员会（General Medical Council）这一公共机构的数据，上个月，美国女性医生人数首次超过男性。

paragraphs.en: Recently, however, the most highly qualified cohort of women in human history has often appeared to be leaning out. In 2024 a study by S&P Global, a compiler of indices, found that women’s share of executive positions in listed American companies fell for the first time in 2023 after nearly 20 years of uninterrupted growth. In 2025 women secured 38% of new board seats at companies in the S&P 500 index of large American firms, down from 42% the previous year and prolonging a retreat from their peak in 2020.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，最近，历史上受过最高教育、资格最强的女性群体似乎常常“lean out”（退出）了。2024年，指数编制机构S&P Global的一项研究发现，上市美国公司中女性担任高管的比例在2023年出现下降，这是近20年来首次中断连续增长。2025年，在美国大型公司的S&P 500指数企业中，女性获得的新董事席位占比为38%，低于上一年的42%，并且延续了从2020年高峰后的回落。

paragraphs.en: In its last round of partner promotions, in 2024, Goldman Sachs named a smaller share of female partners than in the previous round, for the first time in more than a decade. A year later the share of new female managing directors at the Wall Street bank fell, too. Last month Marianne Lake, a female frontrunner to succeed Jamie Dimon at JPMorgan Chase, was relegated and another, Jennifer Piepszak, bowed out last year, leaving an unusually pale and male lineup for the top job.
paragraphs.zh: 在其最近一轮合伙人晋升中，2024年高盛提名的女性合伙人比例比上一轮更低，这是十多年里第一次出现这种情况。一年后，该投行新晋女性董事总经理的比例也下降了。上个月，摩根大通前景看好的女性接班人玛丽安·莱克在与詹姆斯·戴蒙接班竞争中被降级，而另一位候选人詹妮弗·皮普扎克则于去年退出，使得这一最高职位的候选名单前所未有地偏向男性。

paragraphs.en: It is not just at the top that the progress of women in work is stalling or even going into reverse (see chart 1). Across 33 members of the OECD, a club of mostly rich countries, the share of employed women in full-time work declined from 78.1% in 2023 to 76.8% a year later—a small dip but also the first one since PwC, a consultancy, started tracking the data 15 years ago. In March 78.5% of American women aged 25-54 were in the labour force, an all-time high. Since then their participation has fallen by almost a percentage point, a much sharper fall than seen among men.
paragraphs.zh: 女性在职场的进展放缓甚至倒退并非只发生在最高层面（见图1）。在经合组织33个成员国（主要是富裕国家）中，女性全职就业比例从2023年的78.1%降至一年后的76.8%——这一降幅虽然不大，却是自咨询公司普华永道（PwC）在15年前开始跟踪该数据以来首次出现的下滑。2025年3月，25至54岁美国女性的劳动参与率为78.5%，创历史新高。从那时起，她们的参与率下降了近一个百分点，降幅远大于男性。

paragraphs.en: University-educated American women with young families appear to be leading the retreat. Their labour-force participation rate took a turn in 2023 after decades of improvement, and has fallen every year since. Last year marked the sharpest drop in participation by mothers with young children in four decades.
paragraphs.zh: 拥有大学学历且有幼子女的美国女性似乎正成为退场主力。她们的劳动参与率在 2023 年出现转折，结束了数十年的上升趋势，此后每年都在下降。去年是近四十年来，年轻子女母亲劳动参与率下降幅度最大的一年。

paragraphs.en: After consistently closing for years, the gender pay gap is opening up again, too. In America it widened in both 2023 and 2024, the first consecutive two-year decline in 60 years. In 2024 it also widened (on PwC’s slightly different measure) in other rich countries, including Canada and France (see chart 2). For British women in their 40s, who were starting their careers when Ms Sandberg was telling them to lean in, the gap likewise increased.
paragraphs.zh: 曾经持续收窄的性别工资差距也再次被拉开。在美国，这一差距在 2023 年和 2024 年都进一步扩大，创下 60 年来首次连续两年下滑。2024 年，在 PwC 使用的另一种口径下，加拿大、法国等其他富裕国家的差距也同步扩大（见图表2）。对于那些在桑德伯格女士呼吁“Lean In”时正值事业起步阶段的英国40多岁女性来说，这一差距也同样在扩大。

paragraphs.en: It also seems that women’s professional aspirations no longer match men’s. An annual survey published in December 2025 by McKinsey, a consultancy, and Lean In, a non-profit founded by Ms Sandberg and named after her book, asks respondents to indicate their interest in climbing up the corporate ladder. Between 2019 and 2023 the share of both men and women keen on a promotion rose from seven in ten to eight in ten. By 2025 it had risen to nearly nine in ten for men but remained flat for women. For female entry-level workers it was just 69%. PwC also finds a persistent gap in men’s and women’s intentions to ask for a bigger job.
paragraphs.zh: 女性的职业志向似乎也不再与男性同步。麦肯锡与 Lean In（一个由桑德伯格女士创立、以其著作命名的非营利组织）在 2025 年 12 月联合发布的一项年度调查中，受访者被要求表明对“爬上公司管理阶梯”这一升迁目标的兴趣。2019 到 2023 年间，男性和女性中有意争取晋升的比例都从十分之七上升到十分之八。到 2025 年，这一比例在男性中几乎达到十分之九，但女性几乎没有变化。对于女性初级员工而言，这一比例只有 69%。PwC 也发现，在男性和女性是否打算争取更高职位方面，存在持续的差距。

paragraphs.en: One reason for some of these reversals may have to do with the arithmetical aftermath of the covid-19 pandemic. Labour economists caution that disproportionate numbers of low-wage female workers lost their jobs during the lockdowns, artificially narrowing the gender pay gap. Some of the recent widening may reflect those women returning to the labour market. Still, the gap’s expansion half a decade after lockdowns ended suggests something else could be at play.
paragraphs.zh: 这些逆转现象的一部分原因可能与 COVID-19 大流行后留下的“算术效应”有关。劳动经济学家提醒，封城期间，低工资女性劳动者失业人数过多，使性别工资差距被人为压缩。近期差距再度扩大的部分，可能反映了这些女性重新回到劳动力市场。尽管如此，在封锁结束后五年差距仍继续扩大，表明可能还有其他因素在起作用。

paragraphs.en: Difficulty finding child care is another potential culprit. Although nurseries have become more affordable in some rich countries lately thanks to subsidies, supply is often constrained. That means frequent long waiting lists and staff shortages. Germany, considered to have affordable child care, still lacks more than 300,000 nursery places for children under three.
paragraphs.zh: 找不到合适的儿童保育也是另一个潜在原因。虽然在一些富裕国家，由于补贴，托儿所近来变得更负担得起，但供给常常受限，因此经常出现排长队等待，护士和老师也短缺。德国被认为拥有负担得起的托儿服务，但对于三岁以下儿童仍然缺少超过30万个托儿名额。

paragraphs.en: At the same time, especially across swathes of Donald Trump’s America, diversity, equity and inclusion have become dirty words. The federal government is proudly dismantling DEI policies. So, more subtly, are many companies that do not want to cross the alpha-male-in-chief. A few years ago Mr Dimon, then a champion of progressive causes, would probably not have dreamed of an all-male shortlist of names to succeed him. ■
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，尤其是在唐纳德·特朗普时代的美国，"多元性、平等性和包容性"（DEI）已经成了有争议的词。联邦政府正自豪地拆解DEI政策。与此同时，许多不想触碰“alpha-male-in-chief”（首席“阿尔法男”）的公司，也更隐蔽地跟进这种做法。几年前，时任进步议题支持者的迪蒙（Dimon）恐怕根本不会想过要给自己的接班人准备一份全是男性的备选名单。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in economics, finance and markets, sign up to Money Talks, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如欲获取关于经济、金融和市场最重大的新闻进行更专业的分析，请订阅我们的周更付费通讯《Money Talks》。
